items.text: Implement the Domain Repository Architecture per docs/specs/2026-03-16T00-00Z-domain-repository-architecture.md. This spec defines repositories for config, prompts, classes, daemon state, git operations, scaffolding, and a shared tierfs package. Callers work with domain objects, not paths. See the spec for package structure, method signatures, migration path, and testing strategy.
Ensure all code added in this worktree has maximum unit code coverage. The overall goal for the work in this worktree is 94%
Hide or fix selfupdate stub: update command misleads users into thinking they're current. Either implement GitHub releases checking or change the message to say self-update isn't available yet.
Update CLI command spec: docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md lists 39 commands but actual CLI has ~50. Add missing commands from orchestrator, audit, and task subcommands.
Add golangci-lint to make lint: .golangci.yml config exists and CI uses it, but Makefile lint target only runs go vet and gofmt. Wire it up.
Add govulncheck to CI: automated dependency vulnerability scanning in .github/workflows/ci.yml.
Document local filesystem requirement: flock(2) advisory locking not honored on NFS/network filesystems. Add to README and SECURITY.md that .wolfcastle/ must be on a local filesystem.

Property-based test for FindNextTask: verify it always returns an actionable task (not blocked, not complete, not audit when non-audit work remains). Random tree generation similar to propagation_property_test.go.
Fuzz test for scanTerminalMarker: verify it handles adversarial input without panicking. Model output is untrusted.
wolfcastle config show command: display fully resolved config after three-tier merge so users can debug override behavior.
Unknown-field detection in config: json.Unmarshal silently ignores typos like modles. Add post-unmarshal diff or DisallowUnknownFields to warn users.
Issue templates: create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ with bug report and feature request templates.
CODEOWNERS file: map packages to @dorkusprime for automatic review assignment.

Fix global lock leak in tests: start_test.go TestStartCmd_ValidationErrors calls start without setting GlobalLockDir, acquiring the real ~/.wolfcastle/daemon.lock. Every test that invokes the start command must redirect GlobalLockDir to t.TempDir().

Delete deprecated scaffold.go: zero production callers. ScaffoldService replaced it. Remove scaffold.go, scaffold_test.go, deprecated_coverage_test.go, reinit_coverage_test.go, and category_a_test.go's Scaffold/ReScaffold calls.

Doctor should detect parent-child state mismatch: when a parent ChildRef.State says complete but the actual child node state is not_started, doctor reports clean. Add a CHILDREF_STATE_MISMATCH validation category that compares ChildRef.State against the child's actual persisted state. Include a deterministic fix that updates the ChildRef to match reality.
ensure test coverage for new code is over 90%

Refactor pipeline.stages from array to dict. Currently Config.Pipeline.Stages is []PipelineStage, ordered by execution sequence. Change to map[string]PipelineStage keyed by stage name (e.g. 'intake', 'execute'). Add an Order field or use a separate []string for execution ordering. This enables unknown-field detection to recurse into individual stage configs and makes stage lookup O(1) instead of linear scan. Touches: internal/config/types.go (PipelineConfig struct), internal/config/config.go (Defaults), internal/config/validate.go (stage validation), internal/config/merge.go (deep merge semantics for the new shape), internal/daemon/iteration.go and stages.go (stage lookup), internal/pipeline/prompt.go (stage resolution), docs/specs CLI command spec (schema section). Must include a migration in MigrationService for existing config files that use the array format.

Update unknown-field detection to benefit from dict stages. With stages as a dict, diffKeys can recurse into each stage's config naturally (map keys, not array indices). Verify that config typos inside individual stage definitions (e.g. 'promt_file' instead of 'prompt_file') are now caught. Add test cases for unknown fields inside stage configs.

Ensure all new and modified code from the stages-dict refactor has test coverage above 90%. Run go test -coverprofile and check that no modified package dropped below its pre-refactor coverage. Pay particular attention to the migration path (old array configs must load correctly) and the validation changes.

Write a spec for the dict-format pipeline stages refactor. Define: the new JSON schema (map[string]PipelineStage + stage_order []string), the PipelineStage struct changes (Name field removed), merge semantics (stages merges as map per three-tier rules, stage_order replaces as array), migration contract for existing array-format configs, updated validation rules, and consumer impact. This spec is the contract that all implementation and audit work verifies against. Write it to .wolfcastle/docs/specs/ via wolfcastle spec create.

Auto-archive completed nodes. Top-level orchestrators that are fully complete (all children, all audits) auto-archive after a configurable delay (default: 24 hours since last activity). Archived nodes move from system/projects/ to archive/. Status shows active work plus a count line ('N archived nodes'). New flags: status --archived (show only archived), status --all (show everything). New commands: wolfcastle archive add --node <addr> (archive early), wolfcastle archive restore --node <addr> (bring back to active), wolfcastle archive delete --node <addr> (permanently remove). The archive/ directory already exists in the scaffold. Needs: archive state tracking (completion timestamp per node), archive move logic, status rendering changes, CLI commands, config for delay threshold.

Comprehensive documentation review. Verify all docs/humans/ pages match reality, all docs/specs/ are current or marked superseded, CONTRIBUTING.md package map is accurate, README feature list is complete, AGENTS.md critical rules are correct, docs/agents/architecture.md reflects current dependency graph. Major changes since last review: config schema rewritten (stages as dict), 10+ new CLI commands (config show, audit aar, audit report), prompt subdirectory restructure, AARs and spec review as new pipeline concepts, status --detail flag, unknown-field detection, stall detector, selfHeal blocked audit remediation, CHILDREF_STATE_MISMATCH in doctor, archive lifecycle.

Ensure all new and modified code has test coverage above 90%. Run go test -coverprofile and verify no package dropped below its pre-change coverage. Pay particular attention to archive move/restore/delete logic, status rendering with archive flags, and the daemon's auto-archive timer.

Bug: daemon regenerates base/config.json back to array-format stages during operation. init --force writes dict format correctly, but after the daemon runs, the file reverts to array format. Something in the daemon's lifecycle (possibly auto-commit, selfHeal, or Reinit triggered by start.go) is rewriting the config from an old template or serializing the Config struct incorrectly.

config show should support node selection: wolfcastle config show audit to show just the audit section, wolfcastle config show pipeline to show just pipeline config, etc. Currently shows the entire resolved config which is noisy for debugging a specific section.

Config write commands: config set, config unset, config append, config remove. 'config set key value --tier local' writes a key (dot-notation for nested paths, value must be valid JSON including arrays/objects, strings can be bare). 'config unset key --tier local' removes a key (writes null for deletion semantics). 'config append key value --tier local' appends to an array. 'config remove key value --tier local' removes by value from an array. Tier defaults to local. No array indexing. All commands validate the resulting config after writing.
Implement the wolfcastle log command redesign per spec docs/specs/2026-03-21T18-00Z-log-command-design.md. The spec is the source of truth. Read it before planning.

make sure all --- 10001-20260321T21-04Z.jsonl ---

[intake-10001] [intake] Starting
[intake-10001] [intake] Complete (exit=0, 24611 bytes) structures have >= 90% code coverage
make sure all wolfcastle log structures have >= 90% code coverage
Fix replayJSON: streams files instead of loading into memory, decompress .gz files before emitting
Fix followJSON: bypass Record channel and emit raw NDJSON lines to avoid unmarshal/marshal round-trip
Fix --follow with stopped daemon: show last session and exit instead of hanging (spec says no-op)
Replace indexOf with bytes.IndexByte, remove stageLabels identity map, remove reimplemented strings.Contains in tests
Add context.Context to SummaryRenderer.Replay for cancellation parity with other renderers
Extract shared renderer wiring from execute.go and intake.go into a helper function
Fix 15 errcheck violations in internal/logrender: handle or explicitly discard write errors
BUG: replayJSON in internal/logrender/follow.go loads entire files into memory and emits raw gzip bytes for .gz files. Must stream and decompress.
BUG: followJSON round-trips through JSON unmarshal/marshal, reordering keys. Should emit raw NDJSON lines directly.
BUG: wolfcastle log --follow with stopped daemon hangs forever. Spec says show last session and exit.
CLEANUP: Replace indexOf with bytes.IndexByte, remove stageLabels identity map, remove reimplemented strings.Contains in logrender tests
CLEANUP: Add context.Context to SummaryRenderer.Replay, extract shared renderer wiring from execute.go/intake.go, fix 15 errcheck violations

Implement Task Classes: behavioral prompts for every class in the spec.

Reference: docs/specs/2026-03-15T00-04Z-task-classes.md (READ THE ENTIRE SPEC BEFORE PLANNING)

This is a three-phase project. PHASES MUST EXECUTE IN ORDER.

PHASE 1: Refactor the execute prompt and wire two-layer class guidance.
universal.md and coding/default.md already exist in internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. Wire the ContextBuilder to inject BOTH layers: universal.md (always) + the resolved class prompt. For unclassified tasks, inject universal.md + coding/default.md. For classified tasks, inject universal.md + the class prompt. Update execute.md to remove any remaining behavioral guidance that belongs in class prompts.

PHASE 2: Author language, framework, and non-language class prompts.
For every class in the spec tables, create a .md file under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. Language files go in coding/ (coding/go.md, coding/python.md). Framework files go in coding/ subdirectories (coding/typescript/react.md). Non-language classes go directly in classes/ (writing.md, audit.md).

CRITICAL RULES FOR CLASS PROMPTS:
- ZERO wolfcastle-specific content. No terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs, AARs, yielding, or system mechanics.
- Each prompt must be RESEARCHED. Search for official style guides, current tooling, modern paradigms. Do not write from training data alone.
- Coding class prompts REPLACE coding/default.md. Not additive. Complete replacements.
- Codebase conventions take precedence. Use "prefer X" not "always use X."
- 40-80 lines for languages, 30-60 for frameworks.
- The writing class (writing.md) references writing/voice.md for voice guidance.

PHASE 3: Wire defaults into config and validate.
Add a task_classes entry to config.Defaults() for EVERY class that has a prompt file. Wire ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon startup. Add --class validation to task add CLI. Auto-assign Class "audit" to audit tasks at claim time.

ORDERING: Create children in this order: Phase 1 leaf, Phase 2 orchestrator, Phase 3 leaf. Phase 2 MUST complete before Phase 3 starts.

Implement Task Classes: behavioral prompts for every class in the spec.

Reference: docs/specs/2026-03-15T00-04Z-task-classes.md (READ THE ENTIRE SPEC BEFORE PLANNING)

universal.md and coding/default.md already exist at internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. DO NOT recreate them.

This is a three-phase project. PHASES MUST EXECUTE IN ORDER.

PHASE 1: Wire two-layer class guidance in ContextBuilder.
The ContextBuilder must inject TWO sections into every task's iteration context: universal.md (always) + the resolved class prompt. For unclassified tasks: universal.md + coding/default.md. For classified coding tasks: universal.md + coding/<class>.md. For non-coding classified tasks: universal.md + <class>.md. Update execute.md to remove any remaining behavioral guidance that belongs in class prompts. Update the ContextBuilder code, not the prompt files (those are done).

PHASE 2: Author language, framework, and non-language class prompts.
For every class in the spec tables, create a .md file under internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/. Language files go in coding/ (coding/go.md, coding/python.md). Framework files go in coding/ subdirectories (coding/typescript/react.md). Non-language classes go directly in classes/ (writing.md, audit.md).

CRITICAL RULES FOR CLASS PROMPTS:
- ZERO wolfcastle-specific content. No terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs, AARs, yielding, or system mechanics.
- Each prompt must be RESEARCHED. Search for official style guides, current tooling, modern paradigms. Do not write from training data alone.
- Coding class prompts REPLACE coding/default.md. Not additive. Complete replacements.
- Codebase conventions take precedence. Use "prefer X" not "always use X."
- 40-80 lines for languages, 30-60 for frameworks.
- The writing class (writing.md) references writing/voice.md for voice guidance.

PHASE 3: Wire defaults into config and validate.
Add a task_classes entry to config.Defaults() for EVERY class that has a prompt file. Wire ClassRepository.Validate() into daemon startup. Add --class validation to task add CLI. Auto-assign Class "audit" to audit tasks at claim time.

ORDERING: Create children in this order: Phase 1 leaf, Phase 2 orchestrator, Phase 3 leaf. Phase 2 MUST complete before Phase 3 starts.
Fix stale --class flag help text in cmd/task/add.go: says 'e.g., lang-go' but actual class keys use 'coding/go' format

writing/voice.md is unreachable: ClassRepository.Resolve('writing') returns only writing.md. Either writing.md should tell the agent to read writing/voice.md from disk, or the reference should be removed

Fix logs --thoughts and --interleaved: both output raw NDJSON instead of formatted text.

The spec at docs/specs/2026-03-21T18-00Z-log-command-design.md defines the expected output. The summary renderer works correctly. The thoughts and interleaved renderers do not match the spec.

Thoughts renderer (internal/logrender/thoughts.go): should extract the text field from assistant-type NDJSON records and print it as plain text. No stage headers, no timestamps, no JSON.

Interleaved renderer (internal/logrender/interleaved.go): should print stage headers with timestamps and glyphs (▶/✓/✗), with assistant text indented below. Currently dumps raw NDJSON.

Also: the daemon's foreground stdout (the output you see when running wolfcastle start in the terminal) should use the interleaved renderer instead of its own separate PrintHuman calls. The rendering code should exist in one place, shared between wolfcastle log and daemon foreground mode.

Configuration docs overhaul per spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T06-00Z-config-docs-overhaul.md

Read the spec first. It defines four new pages (quickstart, guide, reference, task classes), cross-references, and a deep-linking pass across all human docs. The spec is the source of truth.

Deterministic git activity per spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T07-00Z-deterministic-git.md

Read the spec first. Move all git operations into the daemon. Agent never touches git. Daemon commits after every task (success and failure). Config knobs to control behavior. Staging area preservation. Documentation pass after implementation.

Codebase knowledge files per spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T08-00Z-codebase-knowledge.md

Read the spec first. New artifact type: living knowledge files that accumulate codebase-specific learnings across tasks. Agents read them every iteration and update them when they discover something non-obvious. Token-budgeted with explicit pruning. CLI commands for add/show/prune. ContextBuilder injection.

Template-based file generation per spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T09-00Z-template-file-generation.md

Read the spec first. Move all generated file content (scaffold, ADRs, specs, task markdown) from Go string literals into template files. Extend the existing PromptRepository/tierfs infrastructure, do NOT create a parallel system. Users can override any template via the three-tier system.
Consolidate atomicWriteJSON and AtomicWriteFile in internal/state/io.go. atomicWriteJSON should marshal then call AtomicWriteFile instead of duplicating the temp-file-rename pattern.

Add inline comment to tryCleanStaleLock in internal/state/filelock.go documenting the PID recycling edge case: if the original process dies and a new process gets the same PID within the 50ms polling window, stale detection incorrectly concludes the lock is held. Consequence is timeout, not corruption. Already documented in SECURITY.md but not at the code site.
Update CLI Commands spec at docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md to reflect the current command count (53+). The spec lists 43 commands from the original release.

Extract the validate package's dependency on daemon.DaemonRepository behind an interface. Define a PIDChecker interface (IsAlive, PIDFileExists, RemovePID, etc.) in the validate package and have DaemonRepository implement it. This breaks the lateral coupling between two domain packages and enables validate to be used as a standalone tool.

Add a Troubleshooting section to docs/humans/ covering common first-run issues: stale lock files (daemon.lock exists but no process running), identity not configured (wolfcastle init not run), NFS/network filesystem causing silent corruption (flock not honored), and stale PID files.

Add a wolfcastle config validate command for standalone config checking without starting the daemon. Loads the three-tier config, runs ValidateStructure, reports errors and warnings, exits with appropriate code. Useful for CI and pre-commit validation.

Add structured timing metrics to daemon NDJSON log events. Include a duration_ms field on stage_complete records so consumers don't have to compute deltas from stage_start timestamps. The log command renderers already compute duration for display; this puts it in the structured data too.

Non-daemon stdout flags. STDOUT in non-daemon mode (wolfcastle start --foreground) should not use --interleaved by default. It should accept the same flags as wolfcastle log (--thoughts, --interleaved, --json, default summary) and use the same default renderer (summary). The foreground output path is in internal/daemon/start.go or cmd/daemon/start.go.

Document the git revert workflow in docs/humans/failure-and-recovery.md. Now that the daemon commits code and state together after every iteration, users can recover from bad task outcomes by reverting to the commit before the failed task. Add a section explaining: all state files and code are committed together, git log shows wolfcastle commits with task titles, to retry a failed task revert to the commit before it and restart the daemon. Include a concrete example with git log and git revert commands.

Implement Phase 1 of Parallel Sibling Execution (ADR-095).

Spec: docs/specs/2026-03-23T10-53Z-parallel-sibling-execution.md
ADR: docs/decisions/095-parallel-sibling-execution.md

Phase 1 is "Scope lock infrastructure." It builds the foundation that later phases use for parallel dispatch. No daemon dispatch changes in this phase. All execution remains serial. The goal is to land the scope lock data model, CLI commands, config, and cleanup so that Phase 2 (scope-aware git) and Phase 3 (parallel dispatch) can build on a solid base.

The work breaks down into these pieces:

1. ScopeLockTable type and Store method.
   Add ScopeLockTable and ScopeLock types to internal/state/types.go (see spec "Types" section for exact struct definitions). Add MutateScopeLocks method to internal/state/store.go following the MutateInbox pattern: acquire namespace lock, read scope-locks.json from fixed path in the namespace directory, apply callback, write atomically, release lock. The file path is {namespace-dir}/scope-locks.json. Add a ReadScopeLocks method too (lock-free read, same as ReadIndex). Tests: round-trip write/read, concurrent mutation under lock, conflict detection helpers.

2. ParallelConfig in DaemonConfig.
   Add ParallelConfig struct to internal/config/types.go (Enabled bool, MaxWorkers int). Add Parallel field to DaemonConfig. Set defaults in config.Defaults() (Enabled: false, MaxWorkers: 3). Add validation in internal/config/validate.go: MaxWorkers must be >= 1 when parallel is configured. Tests: defaults populated, validation rejects MaxWorkers < 1, deep merge preserves nested fields.

3. Scope conflict detection helper.
   Add a function (in internal/state/ or a new internal/scope/ package) that implements the bidirectional prefix conflict rule. Two scope entries conflict if either is a prefix of the other. Paths ending in / are directory scopes. "internal/daemon/" conflicts with "internal/daemon/iteration.go" and vice versa. "internal/" conflicts with "internal/daemon/". Tests: table-driven with all the edge cases from the spec's "Directory scope" section.

4. CLI commands: wolfcastle task scope add/list/release.
   Create cmd/task/scope.go (or a scope/ subpackage under cmd/task/). Register a "scope" parent command under taskCmd in cmd/task/register.go with three children: add, list, release. The scope parent has no action. Details for each:
   - add: takes --node (required), --task (optional), and positional file args. Calls MutateScopeLocks. Uses the conflict detection helper. All-or-nothing: if any file conflicts, reject the entire batch and return the conflict in the error envelope. Exit code 0 on success, 1 on conflict. JSON output uses action "task_scope_add". See spec for exact envelope shapes.
   - list: takes optional --node and --task filters. Reads scope lock table (no lock needed). Prints locks as JSON or human-readable table.
   - release: takes --node and --task (both required), optional positional file args. Without files, releases all locks for that task. With files, releases only those.
   Tests: add acquires locks, add detects conflicts, add is idempotent for same task, list filters correctly, release clears locks, release is no-op for missing locks.

5. Stale lock cleanup in selfHeal.
   In internal/daemon/daemon.go selfHeal(), after the existing task healing loop: read the scope lock table. For each lock, check if the PID is alive. The isProcessRunning function in internal/state/filelock.go is unexported; either export it (rename to IsProcessRunning) or add an equivalent check in the daemon package using os.FindProcess + signal 0. Remove locks held by dead processes. If the scope lock file exists but no locks survive cleanup, delete the file. Tests: stale locks from dead PIDs are removed, locks from live PIDs survive, missing scope-locks.json is a no-op.

6. Scope lock display in wolfcastle status.
   When scope locks exist, show them in the status output under each in-progress task that holds locks. Read the scope lock table in showTreeStatus (or printNodeTree) and cross-reference with task addresses. Format: "scope: file1, file2, dir/" indented under the task line. Only show when locks are present; no output change when the table is empty or absent. Tests: status output includes scope lines when locks exist.

7. Gitignore updates.
   Add "scope-locks.json" to both .wolfcastle/.gitignore (the live file in this repo) and internal/project/templates/scaffold/gitignore.tmpl (the template for new projects). Place it in the "Runtime artifacts" section alongside the existing system/wolfcastle.pid, system/stop, and *.lock entries. Tests: scaffold snapshot tests may need updating if they assert on gitignore content.

Read the full spec before starting any task. The spec has exact type definitions, JSON schemas, conflict rules, and envelope formats. Do not invent; implement what the spec says.
